description: A designed informational graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor. The top half is a darkened photograph of President Donald Trump standing in a formal, ornate interior (gilded arches and chandeliers, likely Mar-a-Lago) wearing a dark navy suit, white shirt and red tie with an American-flag lapel pin, looking directly at the camera with a stern expression. Overlaid on the photo in large white serif type is a heading: 'Pell Grants for' (italic) above 'TRADE SCHOOLS!' (bold all-caps). The lower half is a white rounded panel with a small green logo reading 'THE ANNIE E. CASEY FOUNDATION' and two paragraphs of black serif body text explaining the program: 'Pell Grants are the nation's primary federal financial aid program for students from low-income backgrounds. Unlike student loans, these grants do not need to be repaid. Traditionally, Pell Grants have been available only to students enrolled in longer-term degree or certificate programs. New legislation, passed by Congress in summer 2025, expands Pell Grant eligibility to include shorter-term training programs. Beginning July 1, 2026, eligible learners will be able to use funds for programs that lead directly to good jobs - particularly in the fields such as information technology, health care and skilled trades.' The technique combines a presidential authority image with explanatory policy text to promote the administration's 'Make America Skilled Again' workforce agenda. Target audience: workers/students considering vocational training and the general public crediting the administration with the expansion.
notable_text: Pell Grants for TRADE SCHOOLS! | New legislation, passed by Congress in summer 2025, expands Pell Grant eligibility to include shorter-term training programs.
